title: lonicera chrysantha subsp. koehneana
intro: Lonicera chrysantha subsp. koehneana, also known as the Golden Honeysuckle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of China, where it is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving respiratory function. Herbal preparations of Lonicera chrysantha subsp. koehneana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and bronchitis.
